Off Calendar Feb-07-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS FORT MASON CENTER, THE GUARDSMEN'S Motion To Allow Defendants Fort Mason Center And The Guardsmen To File A First Amended Answer To Complaint

No appearance.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted by the court, as follows:

Defendants' motion "to file a first amended answer to complaint" is granted.

No trial date is set and "[t]hat trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP 428.50(c).) A proposed amendment's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported. (302/RBU)
